---
title: "Edible Plants Dataset Analisis (TidyTuesday) - Exploration Data Analysis (EDA)"
author: "Leonardo Cárdenas Peralta, Daniel Enrique Molina Sanglade and Enrique Fernandez Salmeron"
date: "April-May 2026"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    theme: cosmo
    highlight: tango
---

## Creation of new columns from the old ones

```{r}
tornados_clean <- read_csv(here("Data", "tornados_clean.csv"))
```

```{r}
# New columns creation
tornados_clean <- tornados_clean %>%
  # 1. CALCULATED INDICATORS
  mutate(
    victims_total = inj + fat,                                  # 1. Total affected people
    is_deadly = fat > 0,                                        # 2. Binary death indictor
    area_sq_mi = (len * (wid / 1760)),                          # 3. Estimated area
    has_loss = !is.na(loss) & loss > 0                          # 4. Economic damage indicator
  ) %>%
  
  # 2. GROUPED CATEGORIES
  mutate(
    season = case_when(                                         # 5. Grouped by season
      mo %in% c(12, 1, 2) ~ "Winter",
      mo %in% c(3, 4, 5)  ~ "Spring",
      mo %in% c(6, 7, 8)  ~ "Summer",
      mo %in% c(9, 10, 11) ~ "Fall"
    ),
    mag_category = case_when(                                   # 6. Agrupación de intensidad (Escala Fujita) Intensity grouping (Fujita scale)
      mag <= 1 ~ "Weak",
      mag <= 3 ~ "Strong",
      mag > 3  ~ "Violent",
      TRUE     ~ "Unknown"
    )
  ) %>%
  
  # 3. RECODED VARIABLES
  mutate(
    us_region = case_when(                                      # 7. State-region recoding
      st %in% c("CT", "ME", "MA", "NH", "RI", "VT", "NJ", "NY", "PA") ~ "Northeast",
      st %in% c("IL", "IN", "IA", "KS", "MI", "MN", "MO", "NE", "ND", "OH", "SD", "WI") ~ "Midwest",
      st %in% c("AL", "AR", "DE", "FL", "GA", "KY", "LA", "MD", "MS", "NC", "OK", "SC", "TN", "TX", "VA", "WV") ~ "South",
      TRUE ~ "West"
    )
  ) %>%
  
  # 4. TRANSFORMATIONS
  mutate(
    loss_log10 = ifelse(loss > 0, log10(loss), 0),              # 8. Logarithmic transformation of losses
    mag_factor = factor(mag, levels = 0:5, ordered = TRUE)      # 9. Mag factor ordered
  ) %>%
  
  # 5. AGGREGATED VALUES
  group_by(yr, st) %>%
  mutate(
    state_annual_fat_total = sum(fat, na.rm = TRUE),            # 10. Total death by state in a year
    state_annual_avg_mag = mean(as.numeric(mag), na.rm = TRUE)  # 11. Average mag by state in a year
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  
  # 6. EXTRA
  mutate(
    time_of_day = case_when(                                    # 12. Day-time grouping
      lubridate::hour(datetime_utc) >= 6 & lubridate::hour(datetime_utc) < 12 ~ "Morning",
      lubridate::hour(datetime_utc) >= 12 & lubridate::hour(datetime_utc) < 18 ~ "Afternoon",
      lubridate::hour(datetime_utc) >= 18 & lubridate::hour(datetime_utc) < 24 ~ "Evening",
      TRUE ~ "Night"
    )
  )

glimpse(tornados_clean)
```

# Graph creation

```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# --- Chart 1: Annual Evolution of Tornados (1950-2022) ---

# Create the chart
p1 <- tornados_clean %>%
  count(yr) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = yr, y = n)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue", size = 1) + 
  geom_point(color = "darkblue", size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "red", linetype = "dashed", se = FALSE, size = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "Annual evolution of tornados in EE.UU.",
    subtitle = "Registered frequency since 1950 to 2022",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Total number of tornados",
    caption = "Source: NOAA | Analysis by Group 15"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

print(p1)

# Save it in the Outputs folder
ggsave(
  filename = here("Outputs", "tornado_annual_evolution.png"),
  plot = p1,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 1000
)
```


```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# --- Chart 2: Seasonal Frequency of Tornados ---

# Prepare the data grouping by season
p2_data <- tornados_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(season)) %>%
  count(season) %>%
  mutate(season = factor(season, levels = c("Winter", "Spring", "Summer", "Fall")))

# Create the chart
p2 <- ggplot(p2_data, aes(x = season, y = n, fill = season)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = -0.5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Winter" = "#A6CEE3", "Spring" = "#B2DF8A", 
                               "Summer" = "#FDBF6F", "Fall" = "#FF7F00")) +
  labs(
    title = "Tornados per season",
    subtitle = "Total cummulative frequency (1950-2022)",
    x = "Season",
    y = "Number of tornados",
    caption = "Source: NOAA | Analysis by Group 15"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11)
  )

print(p2)

# Save it in the Outputs folder
ggsave(
  filename = here("Outputs", "tornado_seasonl_frequency.png"),
  plot = p2,
  width = 8,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 1000
)
```




```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# --- Chart 3: Daily Cycle of Tornadoes (Frequency by Time of Day) ---

# Prepare data by ordering the time categories
p3_data <- tornados_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(time_of_day)) %>%
  count(time_of_day) %>%
  mutate(time_of_day = factor(time_of_day, 
                              levels = c("Morning", "Afternoon", "Evening", "Night")))

# Create the chart
p3 <- ggplot(p3_data, aes(x = time_of_day, y = n, fill = time_of_day)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = -0.5, fontface = "bold", size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Morning"   = "#FDB462", 
                               "Afternoon" = "#FB8072", 
                               "Evening"   = "#80B1D3", 
                               "Night"     = "#3F51B5")) +
  labs(
    title = "Daily Cycle of Tornado Activity",
    subtitle = "Total frequency by time of day (1950-2022)",
    x = "Time of Day",
    y = "Number of Tornadoes",
    caption = "Source: NOAA | Analysis by Group 15"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

print(p3)

# Save it in the Outputs folder
ggsave(
  filename = here("Outputs", "tornado_daily_cycle.png"),
  plot = p3,
  width = 8,
  height = 5,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 1000
)
```



```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# --- Chart 4: Magnitude vs. Economic Damage (Box Plot) ---

# Prepare the data filtering NAs and categories
p4_data <- tornados_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(mag_category), !is.na(loss_log10)) %>%
  filter(loss_log10 > 0) %>% 
  mutate(mag_category = factor(mag_category, 
                               levels = c("Weak", "Strong", "Violent")))

# Create chart
p4 <- ggplot(p4_data, aes(x = mag_category, y = loss_log10, fill = mag_category)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.colour = "#D32F2F", outlier.shape = 16, outlier.size = 1) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "YlOrRd") +
  labs(
    title = "Economic Damage by Tornado Magnitude",
    subtitle = "Distribution of losses (Log10 scale) across intensity categories",
    x = "Intensity Category (Fujita Scale)",
    y = "Log10 of Economic Loss ($)",
    caption = "Source: NOAA | Analysis by Group 15",
    fill = "Magnitude"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

print(p4)

# Save it in the Outputs folder
ggsave(
  filename = here("Outputs", "tornado_magnitude_damage_boxplot.png"),
  plot = p4,
  width = 8,
  height = 6,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 1000
)
```


```{r data cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# --- Chart 5: Regional Mortality (Stacked Bar Chart) ---

# Prepare the data converting logical to descriptive factor
p5_data <- tornados_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(us_region), !is.na(is_deadly)) %>%
  mutate(
    is_deadly = factor(is_deadly, 
                       levels = c(TRUE, FALSE), 
                       labels = c("Fatal", "Non-Fatal")),
    us_region = factor(us_region, 
                       levels = c("South", "Midwest", "Northeast", "West"))
  )

# Create the chart
p5 <- ggplot(p5_data, aes(x = us_region, fill = is_deadly)) +
  geom_bar(position = "stack", color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Fatal" = "#B71C1C", "Non-Fatal" = "#455A64")) +
  labs(
    title = "Tornado Mortality by US Region",
    subtitle = "Total count of fatal vs. non-fatal events (1950-2022)",
    x = "Geographical Region",
    y = "Total Number of Tornadoes",
    fill = "Outcome",
    caption = "Source: NOAA | Analysis by Group 15"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

print(p5)

# Save it in the Outputs folder
ggsave(
  filename = here("Outputs", "tornado_regional_mortality_stacked.png"),
  plot = p5,
  width = 9,
  height = 6,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 1000
)
```

```{r}
# --- Chart 6: Path Length vs. Total Victims (Scatter Plot with LR) ---

# Prepare the data filtering NAs in variables of interest
p6_data <- tornados_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(len), !is.na(victims_total)) %>%
  filter(len > 0)

# Create the chart
p6 <- ggplot(p6_data, aes(x = len, y = victims_total)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, color = "#2c3e50", size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "#e74c3c", fill = "#f1948a", se = TRUE) +
  labs(
    title = "Correlation Between Path Length and Total Victims",
    subtitle = "Relationship between tornado length (miles) and combined injuries/fatalities",
    x = "Tornado Path Length (miles)",
    y = "Total Victims (Injuries + Fatalities)",
    caption = "Source: NOAA | Analysis by Group 15"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

print(p6)

# Save it in the Outputs folder
ggsave(
  filename = here("Outputs", "tornado_length_victims_scatter.png"),
  plot = p6,
  width = 9,
  height = 6,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 1000
)
```

```{r}
# --- Chart 7: Tornado Heatmap (Density Map of USA) ---

# Load the US map from the "maps" package
us_mainland <- map_data("state")

# Prepare the data filtering coordinates 
p7_data <- tornados_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(slon), !is.na(slat)) %>%
  filter(slon > -125 & slon < -66, 
         slat > 24 & slat < 50)

# Create the chart
p7 <- ggplot() +
  # Base layer: US map
  geom_polygon(data = us_mainland, 
               aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group), 
               fill = "#fdfdfd", color = "#d1d1d1", size = 0.2) +
  # Heatmap layer
  geom_bin2d(data = p7_data, 
             aes(x = slon, y = slat), 
             bins = 80, alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma", name = "Frequency") +
  coord_fixed(1.3) +
  labs(
    title = "Geographic Density of Tornado Touchdowns",
    subtitle = "Heatmap of starting points (slon/slat) across the mainland US",
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude",
    caption = "Source: NOAA | Analysis by Group 15"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#f0f0f0", color = NA), # Light background for contrast
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

print(p7)

# Save it in the Outputs folder
ggsave(
  filename = here("Outputs", "tornado_geographic_heatmap.png"),
  plot = p7,
  width = 12,
  height = 7,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 1000
)
```

```{r}
# --- Chart 8: Top 10 States by Total Fatalities (Flipped Bar Chart) ---

# Prepare the data summing fatalities per state and getting the top 10
p8_data <- tornados_clean %>%
  group_by(st) %>%
  summarise(total_fatalities = sum(fat, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_fatalities)) %>%
  slice_max(total_fatalities, n = 10) %>%
  mutate(st = reorder(st, total_fatalities))

# Create the chart
p8 <- ggplot(p8_data, aes(x = st, y = total_fatalities, fill = total_fatalities)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  coord_flip() +
  geom_text(aes(label = total_fatalities), hjust = -0.2, fontface = "bold", size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#ffcdd2", high = "#b71c1c") +
  labs(
    title = "Top 10 Deadliest States by Tornado Fatalities",
    subtitle = "Total accumulated fatalities (1950-2022)",
    x = "State Abbreviation",
    y = "Total Fatalities",
    caption = "Source: NOAA | Analysis by Group 15"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(), 
    plot.margin = margin(10, 20, 10, 10)
  )

print(p8)

# Save it in the Outputs folder
ggsave(
  filename = here("Outputs", "top_10_deadliest_states.png"),
  plot = p8,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 1000
)
```

```{r}
# --- Chart 9: Correlation Matrix of Numerical Variables ---

# Necessary for the correlation matrix
library(reshape2) 

numeric_vars <- tornados_clean %>%
  select(
    mag, len, wid, inj, fat, 
    loss, victims_total, area_sq_mi, 
    loss_log10
  ) %>%
  drop_na() 

# Calculated matrix
cor_matrix <- cor(numeric_vars, method = "pearson")

melted_cor <- melt(cor_matrix)

# Create the chart
p9 <- ggplot(data = melted_cor, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(value, 2)), color = "black", size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#0571b0", high = "#ca0020", mid = "white", 
    midpoint = 0, limit = c(-1, 1), space = "Lab", 
    name = "Pearson\nCorrelation"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Correlation Matrix of Tornado Metrics",
    subtitle = "Relationship between physical characteristics and impacts",
    x = "", y = "",
    caption = "Source: NOAA | Analysis by Group 15"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank()
  )

print(p9)

# Save it in the Outputs folder
ggsave(
  filename = here("Outputs", "tornado_correlation_matrix.png"),
  plot = p9,
  width = 8,
  height = 8,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 1000  
)
```

```{r}
# --- Chart 10: Magnitude Density by Decade ---

# Prepare the data creating a "decade" column and filtering NAs
p10_data <- tornados_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(mag)) %>%
  mutate(
    decade = floor(yr / 10) * 10,
    decade = paste0(decade, "s"),
    decade = factor(decade)
  )

# Create the chart
p10 <- ggplot(p10_data, aes(x = mag, fill = decade, color = decade)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.1, size = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "viridis", name = "Decade") +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "viridis", name = "Decade") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Tornado Magnitudes by Decade",
    subtitle = "Comparing the density of Fujita Scale ratings (1950-2022)",
    x = "Magnitude (F/EF Scale)",
    y = "Density",
    caption = "Source: NOAA | Analysis by Group 15"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

print(p10)

# Save it in the Outputs folder
ggsave(
  filename = here("Outputs", "tornado_magnitude_density_decade.png"),
  plot = p10,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 1000
)
```
